# Community Health Worker Network — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-community-health-community-health-site
**Vertical slug:** community-health
**VN-ID:** VN-HLT-PHC-001
**Niche family:** NF-HLT-PHC standalone
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's primary healthcare system depends on ~100,000 Community Health Officers (CHOs), Community Health Extension Workers (CHEWs), and Village Health Workers (VHWs) deployed by LGA health authorities and NGOs. They provide immunisation, antenatal care, family planning, malaria treatment, TB screening, and maternal health support. The NPHCDA (National Primary Health Care Development Agency) coordinates funding under the Basic Health Care Provision Fund (BHCPF). Community Health programmes also attract donor funding from UNICEF, WHO, USAID, and bilateral partners. A website serves as a public credibility anchor for funding applications and community trust.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **UNICEF Programme Officer, 38, Abuja** — Vetting community health partners for funding. Needs programme coverage data, CHW count, and quarterly reports.
- **Mama Patience, 55, Community Member, Akwa Ibom** — Wants to know immunisation schedule, antenatal clinic days, and nearest CHW contact.
- **Dr Chidi Nnadi, 44, LGA Health Officer, Enugu** — Supervises CHW deployment. Needs a central portal for network communication and scheduling.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home:** Hero (CHWs in community), programme coverage map, NPHCDA/BHCPF badges, WhatsApp contact
- **Services:** Immunisation, Antenatal Care, Family Planning, Malaria, TB/Nutrition, Health Education
- **Coverage:** LGAs and wards covered, CHW count, health posts supported
- **Reports & Transparency:** Quarterly programme reports, donor acknowledgements
- **About:** Organisation history, CHODANP membership, MDCN oversight physician, CAC registration
- **Contact:** WhatsApp, phone, address, NDPR notice

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Compassionate, transparent, professional. "Bringing healthcare to every doorstep" register. Reference NPHCDA, BHCPF, and LGA health system by name. Primary CTA: "Find a CHW Near You". Secondary CTA: "Partner With Us". WhatsApp: "Talk to a Health Worker".

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- NPHCDA partnership/registration
- CHODANP membership (Community Health Officers and Dental Workers)
- MDCN-registered supervising physician name and licence number
- CAC NGO registration
- Donor logos (UNICEF, WHO, USAID) where applicable
- LGA Ministry of Health partnership letters
- NDPR consent (patient data handling policy)

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. CHW in green uniform conducting home visit — rural Nigerian compound
2. Immunisation session — CHW administering vaccine to infant, mother present
3. Antenatal clinic — pregnant women waiting in community health centre
4. Community health talk — CHW with flipchart, village meeting under tree
5. CHW network team photo — diverse workers in branded uniforms
6. Mobile health outreach — CHW with bicycle/motorbike in rural terrain
7. Child nutrition screening — MUAC measurement, child looking healthy

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- NPHCDA partnership registration
- MDCN registration of supervising physician displayed
- CHODANP membership certificate
- CAC NGO registration
- NDPR notice for patient and community data
- Child data protection policy (for paediatric programmes)

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA on home page and contact page
- [x] All prices/fees in NGN where applicable (some services free under BHCPF)
- [x] Nigerian LGAs/states (Akwa Ibom, Enugu, Borno) as coverage context
- [x] Nigerian names (Mama Patience, Dr Chidi) in content
- [x] NPHCDA trust signal displayed
- [x] Payment: bank transfer, Paystack for donations
- [x] Mobile-first 375px layout
- [x] esc() on all user-supplied strings
- [x] NDPR notice in contact/partner forms
